example2: I make coffee. \br She made a cake. \br It has been made of wood.

title: KNOW /niú/ SABER/ CONHECER
definition: To have information \br irregular \br know; knew; known
example1: I know the answer. \br He knew her name. \br She has known him for years.
example2: I know the answer. \br He knew her name. \br She has known him for years.

title: THINK /fóot/ PENSAR
definition: To have an opinion \br irregular \br think; thought; thought
example1: I think it's good. \br She thought about it. \br He has thought of a plan.
example2: I think it's good. \br She thought about it. \br He has thought of a plan.

title: TAKE /túk/ PEGAR/ LEVAR
definition: To hold or carry \br irregular \br take; took; taken
example1: I take the bus. \br He took a photo. \br The seat has been taken.
example2: I take the bus. \br He took a photo. \br The seat has been taken.

title: SEE /sóo/ VER
definition: To notice with eyes \br irregular \br see; saw; seen
example1: I see a bird. \br We saw a film. \br I have never seen snow.
example2: I see a bird. \br We saw a film. \br I have never seen snow.

title: COME /kêim/ VIR
definition: To move toward the speaker \br irregular \br come; came; come
example1: Come here. \br She came late. \br The guests have come.
example2: Come here. \br She came late. \br The guests have come.
